title: OpenBindings Interface Document (v0.2.0)
description: Derived JSON Schema for the OpenBindings interface document. Expresses the structural portion of the specification; where prose and schema conflict, the prose governs (see openbindings.md §10).

openbindings.description: OpenBindings specification version the document declares. Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 syntax (e.g., 0.2.0). Pattern validates syntax only: whether a processor supports the declared version is explicit implementation support per OBI-T-04 and §8.1, and is not expressible in this schema.

name.description: Human-friendly interface name. Not an identifier.

version.description: Interface-version label: non-empty, author-controlled, opaque to the specification. No ordering, compatibility, or identity semantics.

schemas.description: Named JSON Schemas referenced by operations.

operations.description: Operation registry keyed by operation name.

sources.description: Map of sources: each names its governing binding specification (bindingSpec) and carries a location, embedded content, or both.

bindings.description: Binding entries keyed by binding name, each an author-declared realization connecting an operation to a source.

transforms.description: Named transforms. Each value is a JSONata expression string (JSONata 2.1 per §5.5).

JSONSchema.description: JSON Schema 2020-12 schema: object or boolean form. Top-level constraints from §5.2 are enforced on the object form here ($schema value pinned, $vocabulary forbidden); recursive enforcement (OBI-D-06/07) and meta-schema well-formedness (OBI-D-17) are prose rules beyond this schema.

deprecated.description: Whether the operation is deprecated (migration hint).

tags.description: Organizational labels for grouping and filtering.

aliases.description: Additional names for this operation, equal in standing to its key. The key plus aliases form one flat, document-unique namespace; every name resolves to this operation. A shared contract's qualified name here claims correspondence with that contract's operation.

idempotent.description: Author-attested effect claim: true asserts repetition with equivalent input under the same relevant context adds no intended operation-level effects; false asserts some repetition can; absent claims nothing. Not a retry-safety or cacheability authorization (§5.1).

input.description: Contract on each caller-facing input value. Omit to leave unspecified; literal null is not valid at this position.

output.description: Contract on each successful caller-facing output value. Omit to leave unspecified; literal null is not valid at this position.

OperationExample.description: An example input/output pair for an operation. Positive contract claims: provided values must validate against the corresponding specified schemas where the governing schema graph is document-internal (OBI-D-11). Member presence is distinct from value: an explicitly null member is a provided JSON null value.

bindingSpec.description: Binding specification identifier: exact, opaque, non-empty string denoting the binding specification governing this source and its bindings (e.g., openbindings.openapi@1). Never implicitly dereferenced; no version algebra.

location.description: Binding-specification-defined absolute address associated with the source: an absolute URI or another absolute form the binding specification defines. Never a relative reference.

content.description: Embedded source-artifact representation as any JSON value; the binding specification defines accepted values and their meaning. Member presence is distinct from value: content carrying JSON null is a present member for the location-or-content requirement.

operation.description: Operation key referenced by this binding.

ref.description: Selector identifying a specific target within the governed source, whether an artifact entry, live-surface member, or another target form. Syntax and meaning are the governing binding specification's concern.

preference.description: Author preference signal among bindings of the same operation: higher is more preferred; equal values express no ordering; omission states no preference and is not equivalent to any numeric value. The specification defines no selection algorithm.

deprecated.description: Author recommends migration away from this binding. A deprecated binding remains discoverable and actionable; no ordering relationship with preference is mandated.

inputTransform.description: Per-value transform from a caller-facing operation input value toward the source's expected input representation.

outputTransform.description: Per-value transform from a source output value toward the operation's output contract.

$ref.description: Same-document JSON Pointer reference in literal form to a named transform (e.g., '#/transforms/myTransform').

TransformOrRef.description: Either an inline JSONata expression or a reference to a named transform.
